title: Freeport-McMoRan Approves $3.8bn Expansion of Grasberg Copper Complex
description: Freeport-McMoRan Approves $3.8bn Expansion of Grasberg Copper Complex Financial Times
htmlContent:
Freeport-McMoRan Approves $3.8bn Expansion of Grasberg Copper Complex

By Henry Sanderson, Financial Times • June 5, 2026

NEW YORK / JAKARTA – Freeport-McMoRan, the world's largest listed copper producer, has approved a $3.8 billion expansion of its Grasberg copper and gold complex in Papua, Indonesia, in a major bet that demand for the metal driven by artificial intelligence infrastructure and the electrification of the global economy will keep prices elevated for years to come.

The expansion, which received board approval on Thursday, will extend the life of Grasberg's underground operations and boost annual copper output by an estimated 200,000 tonnes, or roughly 10% of the company's current production, once fully ramped up in 2030.

Chief executive Kathleen Quirk said the investment reflected Freeport's conviction that "the structural case for copper has never been stronger." She noted that the company had seen direct approaches from hyperscale data centre developers seeking to secure long-term copper supply contracts as they raced to build AI infrastructure.

"We are in discussions with several of the largest technology companies in the world about offtake agreements," Ms Quirk said. "The demand signal we are receiving from that sector is extraordinary."

The announcement drove Freeport's shares up 5.2% in pre-market trading in New York. Peers Rio Tinto, BHP and Anglo American also moved higher on the news, which was seen as a bellwether for industry confidence in the copper demand outlook.

Grasberg, one of the world's largest copper and gold deposits, has been mined since the late 1980s. The transition from open-pit to underground block cave mining, completed in 2022, was one of the most complex engineering projects in mining history. The new expansion will open additional ore bodies at depth, requiring new shaft sinking and ventilation infrastructure.

Indonesia's government, which holds a 51% stake in the Grasberg joint venture through state mining company MIND ID, has indicated its support for the expansion, which is expected to generate significant royalty revenues and local employment.

The decision marks a shift in investor attitudes toward large mining capital expenditure. For much of the early 2020s, major miners prioritised returns to shareholders over growth investment following a period of value destruction from overambitious expansion during the previous commodity supercycle.

Tags: Freeport-McMoRan, Grasberg, copper, Indonesia, mining expansion, AI, capex, data center

Sentiment signal: bullish
